title: Files, folders and paths deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Files, folders and paths; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Files, folders and paths: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A relative path always resolves from the user's home folder and a file extension proves the file's format.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Resolve relative paths from the current working directory, normalize segments carefully, and inspect or validate content instead of trusting an extension alone.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Resolve relative paths from the current working directory, normalize segments carefully, and inspect or validate content instead of trusting an extension alone.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Files, folders and paths instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Resolve relative paths from the current working directory, normalize segments carefully, and inspect or validate content instead of trusting an extension alone.
A relative path always resolves from the user's home folder and a file extension proves the file's format.

answer.explanation: Resolve relative paths from the current working directory, normalize segments carefully, and inspect or validate content instead of trusting an extension alone.
